do_not_merge.reason: Per-route rule: different service variants (limited_stop vs local) never merge. Strong identity field: service_variant.

Per-route rule: route_m15-local-limited bundles both local and limited service (description 'Local/Limited route reference'). Even though its payload has limited_stop variant, it cannot be folded into the purely limited record. Do not merge a multi-variant bundle into a single variant.
Per-route rule: different service variants (limited_stop vs sbs) never merge. Strong identity field: service_variant.
Per-route rule: route_m15-local-limited bundles both local and limited service; cannot be merged into the purely local record.
Per-route rule: different service variants (local vs sbs) never merge. Strong identity field: service_variant.

Per-route rule: route_m15-local-limited bundles local/limited and cannot be merged into the SBS variant (different service variant). Strong identity fields: service_variant differing (limited_stop vs sbs) and bundling nature.

needed_context: The record description 'M15 Local/Limited route reference' indicates it may represent both variants. Evidence from sources like speeding_up_slowly_2025 suggests a generic reference. Determine whether this record should be a distinct canonical entity representing the local/limited combined service (like a route category) or whether it should be split into separate variant records. Until evidence pins a single variant or confirms it as a category, it remains ambiguous.

suggestion.rationale: All cross-variant pairs have different service_variant values or represent a multi-variant bundle, which per the route merge canon never merge. The limited and local records are clearly distinct variants. The bundle record route_m15-local-limited is ambiguous in its variant representation but cannot be merged with any single variant record. No merge groups are valid. The do_not_merge decisions are forced by test 1 (contradiction in strong identity field service_variant) and the per-kind rule about multi-variant bundles.
